sn35.118:1.1: On one occasion the Blessed One was staying near Rājagaha on Vulture Peak Mountain.
sn35.118:1.2: Then Sakka the deva-king went to the Blessed One and, on arrival, having bowed down to him, stood to one side. As he was standing there, he said to the Blessed One,
sn35.118:1.3: “What is the cause, lord, what is the reason why some beings don’t totally unbind in the here & now?
sn35.118:1.4: And what is the cause, what is the reason why some beings do totally unbind in the here & now?”
sn35.118:2.1: “There are, deva-king, forms cognizable via the eye—agreeable, pleasing, charming, endearing, enticing, linked with sensual desire.
sn35.118:2.2: If a monk relishes them, welcomes them, & remains fastened to them,
sn35.118:2.3: then in him—relishing them, welcoming them, & remaining fastened to them—consciousness depends on them, clings to them/is sustained by them.
sn35.118:2.4: A monk with clinging/sustenance doesn’t totally unbind. “There are sounds cognizable via the ear… aromas cognizable via the nose…
sn35.118:3.1: flavors cognizable via the tongue… tactile sensations cognizable via the body…
sn35.118:3.2: “There are ideas cognizable via the intellect—agreeable, pleasing, charming, endearing, enticing, linked with sensual desire.
sn35.118:3.3: If a monk relishes them, welcomes them, & remains fastened to them,
sn35.118:3.4: then in him—relishing them, welcoming them, & remaining fastened to them— consciousness depends on them, clings to them/is sustained by them.
sn35.118:3.5: A monk with clinging/sustenance doesn’t totally unbind.
sn35.118:3.6: “This, deva-king, is the cause, this the reason, why some beings don’t totally unbind in the here & now.
sn35.118:4.1: “But, deva-king, there are forms cognizable via the eye—agreeable, pleasing, charming, endearing, enticing, linked with sensual desire.
sn35.118:4.2: If a monk doesn’t relish them, welcome them, or remain fastened to them,
sn35.118:4.3: then in him—not relishing them, not welcoming them, not remaining fastened to them— consciousness doesn’t depend on them, doesn’t cling to them/isn’t sustained by them.
sn35.118:4.4: A monk without clinging/sustenance totally unbinds. “There are sounds cognizable via the ear… aromas cognizable via the nose…
sn35.118:5.1: flavors cognizable via the tongue… tactile sensations cognizable via the body…
sn35.118:5.2: “There are ideas cognizable via the intellect—agreeable, pleasing, charming, endearing, enticing, linked with sensual desire.
sn35.118:5.3: If a monk doesn’t relish them, welcome them, or remain fastened to them,
sn35.118:5.4: then in him—not relishing them, not welcoming them, not remaining fastened to them—consciousness doesn’t depend on them, doesn’t cling to them/isn’t sustained by them.
sn35.118:5.5: A monk without clinging/sustenance totally unbinds.
sn35.118:5.6: “This, deva-king, is the cause, this the reason, why some beings do totally unbind in the here & now.”
